raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A request to improve a research draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and stated distinction from prior routing methods.","R1.2":"The artifact contains an apparent internal inconsistency between the claimed buffer-free design and the proof's reliance on a validation buffer, making argument-chain repair central.","R1.3":"Improving the draft includes checking whether empirical and theoretical claims are supported by the reported benchmark and proof statements.","R1.5":"A broad draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level narrative order and how motivation, formalism, theory, and experiments build on each other.","R1.6":"Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for better writing quality.","RX.1":"Any projection of this broad request must preserve uncertainty about which specific improvement mode is intended.","RX.2":"The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the artifact's stated evidence and assumptions justify.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated review, experiments, or literature searches unless requested.","RX.4":"The projection must avoid downstream harm such as encouraging unsupported scientific claims or misleading descriptions of the method.","RX.5":"Traceability is required so proposed responsibilities remain anchored to the delegation and artifact rather than invented external context."}}

rationale.R1.1: A request to improve a research draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution framing, and stated distinction from prior routing methods.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an apparent internal inconsistency between the claimed buffer-free design and the proof's reliance on a validation buffer, making argument-chain repair central.
rationale.R1.3: Improving the draft includes checking whether empirical and theoretical claims are supported by the reported benchmark and proof statements.
rationale.R1.5: A broad draft-improvement request reasonably includes improving section-level narrative order and how motivation, formalism, theory, and experiments build on each other.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level polish is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' most directly asks for better writing quality.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of this broad request must preserve uncertainty about which specific improvement mode is intended.
rationale.RX.2: The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the artifact's stated evidence and assumptions justify.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated review, experiments, or literature searches unless requested.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must avoid downstream harm such as encouraging unsupported scientific claims or misleading descriptions of the method.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is required so proposed responsibilities remain anchored to the delegation and artifact rather than invented external context.
